title: Ukraine seeks to broaden diplomatic horizon by selling arms
subhead: The conflict in Iran allows Kyiv to showcase the technologies and expertise developed since the Russian invasion of 2022, forging partnerships with Gulf states and countries in the Caucasus.

UK PM Starmer fights for political survival as calls for his resignation grow
subhead: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he had no plans to resign on Tuesday after facing growing calls within his party to step down following dismal general election results for Labour.

title: Strait of Hormuz: UN seeks to secure fertilizer shipments to fragile countries to prevent famines
subhead: The United Nations has warned that vulnerable countries across Africa and Asia might experience fertilizer shortages during the crucial planting season. An estimated 45 million people face the threat of falling to hunger.

title: Stars descend on the carpet as Cannes Film Festival 2026 opens without Hollywood backing
subhead: In the main Cannes competition, which begins Wednesday, 22 films are vying for the prestigious Palme d'Or prize for best film. As usual in the build-up to the festival, off-screen events have dominated conversation, from the impact of AI on jobs, Hollywood's decision to ghost the event, to the under-representation of women directors.

title: Eye Haïdara, master of ceremonies at the Cannes Film Festival: 'When you're on stage, you're in charge! You're untouchable'
subhead: The actress, who rose to fame in France in 2017, paid tribute to her primary school teacher, who inspired her passion for theater.

In Spain, the woman priest challenging the Vatican
subhead: Christina Moreira advocates for women's right to the priesthood, and was excommunicated by the Vatican for seeking the role. She continues her vocation as a parish priest in Galicia, disturbing figures high up in the Catholic Church.

title: After a decade of crises between France and its former colonies, Macron chooses Kenya for the last major African trip of his presidency
subhead: Coming to power having promised to rebuild the relationship between France and its former colonies, Emmanuel Macron had to face a series of crises, particularly in the Sahel region. In the English-speaking part of the continent, Paris struggled to find its place.

title: Inside the mutually beneficial relationship of shipping magnate Saadé and President Macron
subhead: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (1/4) The French leader and the head of the company CMA CGM have developed a relationship that is as respectful as it is useful for both parties, given how closely the interests of the family-owned group and the French state are intertwined.
